Reference_title: ZmERF21 directly regulates hormone signaling and stress-responsive gene expression to influence drought tolerance in maize seedlings
Description: Overexpression of ZmERF21 in maize significantly increased the chlorophyll content and activities of antioxidant enzymes under drought conditions. ZmERF21 promotes drought tolerance in maize.

Synonyms: ABI5, ABSCISIC ACID-INSENSITIVE 5-like protein 5, ZmABF3, auxin-responsive protein, GRMZM2G157722
Reference_title: Maize ZmbZIP33 is involved in drought resistance and recovery ability through an abscisic acid-dependent signaling pathway
Description: Overexpression of ZmbZIP33 remarkably increased chlorophyll content and root length after drought stress and rewatering, and, moreover, cause an accumulation of ABA content, thereby improving drought resistance and recovery ability in Arabidopsis. ZmbZIP33 played roles in ABA biosynthesis and regulation of drought response and rewatering in Arabidopsis and maize through an ABA-dependent signaling pathway.

Reference_title: Overexpression of maize ZmHDZIV14 increases abscisic acid sensitivity and mediates drought and salt stress in Arabidopsis and tobacco
Description: Overexpression of ZmHDZIV14 in Arabidopsis caused increased sensitivity to abscisic acid (ABA), tolerance of salt and mannitol at germination stage, and drought tolerance of seedlings. ZmHDZIV14 transgenic Arabidopsis lines placed under drought stress additionally displayed lower levels of malondialdehyde, higher proline, and relative water content, compared to the wild-type.

Reference_title: The maize AP2/EREBP transcription factor zmereb160 enhances drought tolerance in Arabidopsis
Description: Overexpression of ZmEREB160 in Arabidopsis significantly enhanced tolerance to osmotic and ABA stress, overexpressed seedlings were longer roots under ABA and mannitol treatments compared with wild type. In addition, overexpression of ZmEREB160 Arabidopsis seedlings was found to elevate survival rate compared with wild type plants under drought stress.

Reference_title: Enhanced vitamin C production mediated by an ABA-induced PTP-like nucleotidase improves drought tolerance of arabidopsis and maize
Description: Overexpressing maize PTPN (ZmPTPN) promoted plant drought tolerance, indicating conserved and positive roles of PTPN in plant drought tolerance. encodes proten with nucleotidase activity; is activated by ABA signaling and is a key regulator of drought-promoted vitamin C production

Reference_title: ZmSRL5 is involved in drought tolerance by maintaining cuticular wax structure in maize
Description: ZmSRL5 is required for the structure formation of the cuticular wax and could increase the drought tolerance by maintaining the proper cuticular wax structure in maize. ZmSRL5 encodes a CASPARIAN‐STRIP‐MEMBRANE‐DOMAIN‐LIKE (CASPL) protein located in plasma membrane, and highly expressed in developing leaves.

Reference_title: The maize class-I SUMO conjugating enzyme ZmSCE1d is involved in drought stress response
Description: Over-expression of ZmSCE1d in transgenic Arabidopsis plants increased SUMO conjugates and improved drought tolerance. The ZmSCE1d-transgenic plants showed higher antioxidant enzyme activities, but lower reactive oxygen species and lipid peroxidation upon drought stress.

Synonyms: nactf109, nac109, Zma003086, ZmNAC11, ZmSNAC8, GRMZM2G014653, ZmSNAC06
Reference_title: Function analysis of ZmNAC33, a positive regulator in drought stress response in Arabidopsis
Description: In ZmNAC33 transgenic Arabidopsis, germination rates were higher than in wild type plants under ABA and osmotic stress at the germination stage, and overexpression lines exhibited higher survival rates and higher antioxidant enzyme activities compared with wild type under drought stress.

Reference_title: Expression of the maize MYB transcription factor ZmMYB3R enhances drought and salt stress tolerance in transgenic plants
Description: Transgenic Arabidopsis plants overexpressing ZmMYB3R displayed enhanced growth performance and higher survival rates, elevated catalase (CAT), peroxidase (POD) and superoxide dismutase (SOD) enzyme activities, increased sensitivity to ABA, and regulation of the stomatal aperture, suggesting that ZmMYB3R enhances tolerance to drought and salt stress.

Reference_title: ZmASR3 from the maize ASR gene family positively regulates drought tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis
Description: Overexpression of maize ZmASR3 gene in Arabidopsis showed lower malondialdehyde (MDA) levels and higher relative water content (RWC) and proline content than the wild type under drought conditions, demonstrating that ZmASR3 can improve drought tolerance. ZmASR3-overexpressing transgenic lines displayed increased stomatal closure and reduced reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation by increasing the enzyme activities of superoxide dismutase (SOD) and catalase (CAT) under drought conditions.

Reference_title: A LEA gene from a Vietnamese maize landrace can enhance the drought tolerance of transgenic maize and tobacco
Description: During drought simulation conditions, the ZmLEA14tv-expressing plants of tobacco showed improved recovery ability, while those of maize enhanced the seed germination in comparison with the non-transgenic control plants.

Reference_title: Over-expression of a maize N-acetylglutamate kinase gene (ZmNAGK) improves drought tolerance in tobacco
Description: Expression of ZmNAGK in tobacco resulted in higher tolerance to drought. Overexpression of ZmNAGK could enhance the activities of antioxidant defense enzymes, and decrease malondialdehyde content and leakage of electrolyte in tobacco under drought stress. In addition, the ZmNAGK transgenic tobaccos activated drought responses.

Synonyms: tcptf38, tcp38, transcription factor PCF8, GRMZM2G180568
Reference_title: Genome-wide analysis of TCP family genes in Zea mays L. identified a role for ZmTCP42 in drought tolerance
Description: Overexpression of ZmTCP42 in Arabidopsis led to a hypersensitivity to ABA in seed germination and enhanced drought tolerance

Reference_title: Roles of a maize phytochrome-interacting factors protein ZmPIF3 in regulation of drought stress responses by controlling stomatal closure in transgenic rice without yield penalty
Description: ZmPIF3 can enhance water-saving and drought-resistance by decreasing stomatal aperture and reducing transpiration in both transgenic rice and transgenic Arabidopsis. ZmPIF3 is a positive regulator of ABA signaling and enhances water-saving and drought-resistance traits by reducing stomatal openings to control water loss.

Reference_title: The Maize WRKY Transcription Factor ZmWRKY40 Confers Drought Resistance in Transgenic Arabidopsis
Description: Overexpression of ZmWRKY40 improved drought tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis by regulating stress-related genes, and the reactive oxygen species (ROS) content in transgenic lines was reduced by enhancing the activities of peroxide dismutase (POD) and catalase (CAT) under drought stress.

Synonyms: SnRK2 serine threonine protein kinase8, snrkII8, ZmSnRK2.3, SAPK8, GRMZM2G138861
Reference_title: ZmOST1 mediates abscisic acid regulation of guard cell ion channels and drought stress responses
Description: ZmOST1 enhances plant drought tolerance through stomata closure, in response to both ABA and water stress signals, thereby reducing water loss.

Reference_title: A maize phytochrome-interacting factors protein ZmPIF1 enhances drought tolerance by inducing stomatal closure and improves grain yield in Oryza sativa
Description: ZmPIF1 plays an important role in the ABA-mediated regulation of stomatal closure to control water loss. The ZmPIF1 transgenic rice and Arabidopsis displayed water saving and drought resistance, which were associated with reduced a stomatal aperture and transpiration rate.

Synonyms: bzip100, bZIP transcription factor 23, bZIP transcription factor 46, ABSCISIC ACID-INSENSITIVE 5-like protein 6,  GRMZM2G033413
Reference_title: Maize ABP2 enhances tolerance to drought and salt stress in transgenic Arabidopsis
Description: Constitutive expression of ABP2 in transgenic Arabidopsis plants enhanced tolerance to drought and salt stress, and increased sensitivity to ABA.

Synonyms: xer1, putative RING zinc finger domain superfamily protein (per NCBI), RING-H2 finger protein, GRMZM2G018070
Reference_title: Overexpression of RING domain E3 ligase ZmXerico1 confers drought tolerance through regulation of ABA homeostasis
Description: Overexpression of ZmXerico1 and ZmXerico2 in maize results in increased ABA levels and decreased levels of ABA degradation products diphaseic acid and phaseic acid. Over-expression improves water use efficiency and drought tolerance in transgenic maize plants through reduction in ABA8OX proteins stability and ABA degradation.

Synonyms: xer2, putative RING zinc finger domain superfamily protein (per NCBI), putative RING zinc finger domain superfamily protein, GRMZM2G393349
Reference_title: Overexpression of RING domain E3 ligase ZmXerico1 confers drought tolerance through regulation of ABA homeostasis
Description: Overexpression of ZmXerico1 and ZmXerico2 in maize results in increased ABA levels and decreased levels of ABA degradation products diphaseic acid and phaseic acid. Over-expression improves water use efficiency and drought tolerance in transgenic maize plants through reduction in ABA8OX proteins stability and ABA degradation.

Synonyms: ZmSnRK3.9, CBL-interacting serine/threonine-protein kinase 8, cipk8, GRMZM2G383240
Reference_title: ZmCIPK8, a CBL-interacting protein kinase, regulates maize response to drought stress
Description: Over-expression of ZmCIPK8 in tobacco induced the expression of the NAC, CBF, and Rd29A genes and enhanced drought tolerance of transgenic tobacco seedlings.

Reference_title: Overexpression of maize SDD1 (ZmSDD1) improves drought resistance in Zea mays L. by reducing stomatal density
Description: ZmSDD1 enhanced the drought resistance in the transgenic maizes through improving WUE and photosynthetic rate which was considered as the result of the higher PEPCase activity, CO2 utilization rate and the lower CO2 compensation point.

Gene name: Zea mays auxin regulated gene involved in organ size
Reference_title: Over-expression of ARGOS genes modifies plant sensitivity to ethylene, leading to improved drought tolerance in both Arabidopsis and maize
Description: In Arabidopsis, over-expression of maize ARGOS1 (Zm-ARGOS1), Zm-ARGOS8, Arabidopsis ARGOS-Like2 (At-ARL2) and At-ARL3 reduced plant sensitivity to ethylene, leading to enhanced drought tolerance.

Gene name: Zea mays auxin regulated gene involved in organ size
Synonyms: zar8, aluminum-induced protein superfamily pseudogene, ARGOS8, GRMZM2G354338
Reference_title: Over-expression of ARGOS genes modifies plant sensitivity to ethylene, leading to improved drought tolerance in both Arabidopsis and maize
Description: In Arabidopsis, over-expression of maize ARGOS1 (Zm-ARGOS1), Zm-ARGOS8, Arabidopsis ARGOS-Like2 (At-ARL2) and At-ARL3 reduced plant sensitivity to ethylene, leading to enhanced drought tolerance.

Reference_title: Expression of maize heat shock transcription factor gene ZmHsf06 enhances the thermotolerance and drought-stress tolerance of transgenic Arabidopsis
Description: Overexpressing ZmHsf06 Arabidopsis plants have enhanced basal and acquired thermotolerance, stronger drought-stress tolerance and growth advantages under mild heat stress conditions.

Gene name: homeodomain-leucine zipper I (HD-Zip) I transcription factor
Reference_title: A novel maize homeodomain-leucine zipper (HD-Zip) I gene, Zmhdz10, positively regulates drought and salt tolerance in both rice and Arabidopsis.
Description: Overexpression of Zmhdz10 in rice led to enhanced tolerance to drought and salt stresses and increased sensitivity to ABA. Moreover, Zmhdz10 transgenic plants had lower relative electrolyte leakage (REL), lower malondialdehyde (MDA) and increased proline content relative to wild-type plants under stress conditions, which may contribute to enhanced stress tolerance.

Reference_title: Overexpression of the phosphatidylinositol synthase gene (ZmPIS) conferring drought stress tolerance by altering membrane lipid composition and increasing ABA synthesis in maize
Description: Under drought stress conditions, the overexpression of ZmPIS in maize resulted in significantly elevated levels of most phospholipids and galactolipids in leaves compared with those in wild type (WT).

Reference_title: A maize calcium-dependent protein kinase gene, ZmCPK4, positively regulated abscisic acid signaling and enhanced drought stress tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis
Description: Overexpression of ZmCPK4 in the transgenic Arabidopsis enhanced ABA sensitivity in seed germination, seedling growth and stomatal movement. The transgenic plants also enhanced drought stress tolerance.

Synonyms: ereb137, ZmDREB2.7/ZmERF18, ethylene-responsive transcription factor ABI4, GRMZM2G028386
Reference_title: Genome-Wide Analysis of ZmDREB Genes and Their Association with Natural Variation in Drought Tolerance at Seedling Stage of Zea mays L
Description: The transgenic Arabidopsis overexpressing ZmDREB2.7 displayed enhanced tolerance to drought stress.

Synonyms: ZmGRF24, grf1, growth-regulating factor 1-like, gbf14, 4-3-3- like protein GF14-6, GRMZM2G102499
Reference_title: Expression of the maize ZmGF14-6 gene in rice confers tolerance to drought stress while enhancing susceptibility to pathogen infection
Description: ZmGF14-6 positively regulates drought tolerance in transgenic rice while negatively modulating the plant defence response to pathogen infection.

Reference_title: Characterization of the ZmDBP4 gene encoding a CRT/DRE-binding protein responsive to drought and cold stress in maize
Description: AP2/ERF transcription factor; Overexpression of ZmDBP4 improved drought and cold-stress tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis plants.

Reference_title: Enhanced expression of phospholipase C 1 (ZmPLC1) improves drought tolerance in transgenic maize
Description: Under drought stress conditions, the sense transgenic plants had higher relative water content, better osmotic adjustment, increased photosynthesis rates, lower percentage of ion leakage and less lipid membrane peroxidation, higher grain yield than the WT

Gene name: CAAT-box DNA binding protein subunit B (NF-YB)
Synonyms: CCAAT-DR1-transcription factor 17, ZmNF-YB11, ZmNF-YB13, ZmNF-YB17, GRMZM5G804893
Reference_title: Plant nuclear factor Y (NF-Y) B subunits confer drought tolerance and lead to improved corn yields on water-limited acres
Description: Under water-limited conditions, transgenic maize plants with increased ZmNF-YB2 expression show tolerance to drought based on the responses of a number of stress-related parameters, including chlorophyll content, stomatal conductance, leaf temperature, reduced wilting, and maintenance of photosynthesis.

Synonyms: nactf49, NAC-transcription factor 49, ZmNAC1, ZmNAC102, ZmNAC010312, GRMZM2G347043
Reference_title: Overexpressing a NAM, ATAF, and CUC (NAC) transcription factor enhances drought resistance and salt tolerance in rice
Description: Overexpression of stress responsive gene SNAC1 significantly enhances drought resistance in transgenic rice (22-34% higher seed setting than control) in the field under severe drought stress conditions at the reproductive stage while showing no phenotypic changes or yield penalty. SNAC1 functions by regulating the expression of genes that contain the NAC Recognized Sequence (NACRS) within their promoter region. SNAC1 is induced predominantly in guard cells by drought and encodes a NAM, ATAF, and CUC (NAC) transcription factor with transactivation activity.

Reference_title: ZmC2H2-149 negatively regulates drought tolerance by repressing ZmHSD1 in maize.
Description: ZmHSD1 is a positive drought tolerance regulator. Overexpression of ZmHSD1 increased drought tolerance and grain yield under water deficit conditions compared to their respective wild-type plants.

Reference_title: ZmC2H2-149 negatively regulates drought tolerance by repressing ZmHSD1 in maize.
Description: Overexpression of ZmC2H2-149 in maize led to a decrease in both drought tolerance and crop yield. ZmC2H2-149 directly suppresses the expression of a positive drought tolerance regulator, ZmHSD1 (hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase 1).

Synonyms: aasr1, aba1, abscisic stress protein homolog, GRMZM2G136910
Reference_title: ZmASR1 negatively regulates drought stress tolerance in maize.
Description: ZmASR1 knockout lines showed enhanced drought stress tolerance in maize. ZmASR1 knockout lines showed lower ROS accumulation, higher ABA content, and a higher degree of stomatal closure than wild-type plants.

Synonyms: HDT3, Zm-HD2c, hda104, hda105, HD2c, GRMZM2G159032
Reference_title: ZmHDT103 Negatively Regulates Drought Stress Tolerance in Maize Seedlings
Description: Under drought conditions, the seedlings of ZmHDT103 mutants exhibited significantly lower water loss rate (WLR), relative electrolytic leakage (REL), hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) level, and malonaldehyde (MDA) level than those of their wild-type (WT) counterparts. Additionally, the seedlings of ZmHDT103 mutants exhibited significantly higher levels of abscisic acid (ABA), relative water content (RWC), peroxidase (POD), and proline (Pro) than those of the WT control.

Reference_title: Maize ZmLAZ1-3 gene negatively regulates drought tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Description: The ectopic expression of the ZmLAZ1-3 gene in Arabidopsis significantly reduced germination ratio and root length, decreased biomass, and relative water content, but increased relative electrical conductivity and malondialdehyde content under drought stress. ZmLAZ1-3 gene is a negative regulator in regulating drought tolerance and can be used to improve maize drought tolerance via its silencing or knockout.

Synonyms: BZR-transcription factor 4, ZmNHL44, beta-amylase 2 chloroplastic , beta-amylase 7, ZmBAM7, GRMZM2G446515
Reference_title: Maize ZmBES1/BZR1-1 transcription factor negatively regulates drought tolerance
Description: Under drought stress, overexpression of ZmBES1/BZR1-1 resulted in the enhancement of drought sensitivity of transgenic Arabidopsis and rice with a lower survival rate, reactive oxygen species (ROS) level and relative water content (RWC) and a higher stomatal aperture and relative electrolyte leakage (REL).  The expression of ZmBES1/BZR1-1 exhibited no tissue specificity but drought-inhibitory expression in maize.

Synonyms: ZmPP2C44, ZmPP2C47, ZmPP2C94, prh94, protein phosphatase homolog94, GRMZM5G829894
Reference_title: The clade F PP2C phosphatase ZmPP84 negatively regulates drought tolerance by repressing stomatal closure in maize.
Description: transcription of ZmPP84 is suppressed by drought stress. ZmPP84 is a negative regulator of drought stress response that inhibits stomatal closure through dephosphorylating ZmMEK1, thereby repressing ZmMEK1-ZmSIMK1 signaling pathway.

Synonyms: ZmCP14, ccp18, cysteine protease18, Senescence-specific cysteine protease SAG12, GRMZM2G070011
Reference_title: A Novel Senescence-Specific Gene (ZmSAG39) Negatively Regulates Darkness and Drought Responses in Maize
Description: Under drought stress, compared with WT plants, the ZmSAG39 knockout lines had a higher seed germination rate, seedling survival rate and chlorophyll content, and lower reactive oxygen species (ROS) level and malondialdehyde (MDA) content. ZmSAG39 negatively regulated some stress-related genes but positively regulated senescence-related genes under darkness and drought stress conditions.

Reference_title: ZmLBD5, a class-II LBD gene, negatively regulates drought tolerance by impairing abscisic acid synthesis
Description: ZmLBD5 is mainly involved in regulation of the TPS-KS-GA2ox gene module, which comprises key enzymatic genes upstream of GA and ABA biosynthesis. The insufficiency of ABA increases stomatal density and aperture in overexpressed plants, and causes the drought sensitive phenotype by promoting water transpiration.

Synonyms: ZmHSFA2-4, hsftf1, heat stress transcription factor B-1, GRMZM2G002131
Reference_title: A novel heat shock transcription factor (ZmHsf08) negatively regulates salt and drought stress responses in maize
Description: Overexpression of ZmHsf08 in maize resulted in enhanced sensitivity to salt and drought stresses, displaying lower survival rates, higher reactive oxygen species (ROS) levels, and increased malondialdehyde (MDA) contents compared with wild-type (WT) plants.

Reference_title: Maize NCP1 negatively regulates drought and ABA responses through interacting with and inhibiting the activity of transcription factor ABP9
Description: NCP1 over-expressing Arabidopsis plants exhibited a reduced sensitivity to ABA and decreased drought tolerance. NCP1, a NINJA family protein lacking EAR motif, acts as a negative regulator of ABA signaling by interacting with and inhibiting the activity of transcriptional activator ABP9.

Synonyms: proline-rich receptor-like protein kinase PERK7, GRMZM2G374074
Reference_title: Ectopic Expression of a Maize Gene ZmDUF1645 in Rice Increases Grain Length and Yield, but Reduces Drought Stress Tolerance
Description: enhanced expression significantly altered various traits in transgenic rice plants, including increased grain length, width, weight, and number per panicle, resulting in a significant increase in yield, but a decrease in rice tolerance to drought stress; may regulate grain size and affect yield through the cytokinin signaling pathway

Reference_title: ZmDST44 Gene Is a Positive Regulator in Plant Drought Stress Tolerance
Description: encodes diphosphoribulose carboxylase in chloroplasts. Overexpression of ZmDST44 in Arabidopsis, rice, and maize resulted in significant enhancements in drought tolerance. Transgenic plants exhibited reduced malondialdehyde (MDA) levels, increased proline accumulation, and upregulation of drought-responsive genes compared to wild-type plants. Transgenic Arabidopsis and rice showed improved drought resistance and higher post-drought recovery rates, and transgenic maize displayed lower sensitivity to drought stress.

Reference_title: Functional analysis of maize GRAS transcription factor gene ZmGRAS72 in response to drought and salt stresses.
Description: ZmGRAS72 significantly improves Arabidopsis thaliana tolerance to drought and salt stresses. ZmGRAS72 regulates NCED3, ABI1, ABI2, ABI4, ABI5, RD22, RD29A and KIN1 under abiotic stress. ZmGRAS72 is involved in maize response to abiotic stress.

Reference_title: ZmNHL2 enhances drought tolerance by regulating the expression of stress-responsive genes and ABA signaling pathway in maize.
Description: ZmNHL2 contains stress response cis-regulatory elements and ABRE response elements and has positive responses to drought, high temperature, salt stress, and exogenous ABA treatment. ZmNHL2-overexpression lines showed upright leaves and less wilting and yellowing. Moreover, the relative water content (RWC), and the activities of superoxide dismutase (SOD) and peroxidase (POD) in the ZmNHL2-overexpression transgenic Arabidopsis and maize plants were higher than that of the WT plants, indicating that the overexpression of ZmNHL2 enhances maize tolerance to drought stress. ZmNHL2-overexpression transgenic plants exhibited higher expression levels of the drought-responsive genes ZmPOD1 and ZmDREB2A, and the ABA-related genes ZmNCED and ZmABF2 under drought-stressed conditions.

Synonyms: nactf118, ZmSNAC11, NAC transcription factor NAM-B2
Reference_title: A novel NAC transcription factor ZmNAC55 negatively regulates drought stress in Zea mays.
Description: negatively regulates drought stress; underexpression in maize enhanced drought tolerance with lower water loss, higher survival rate, lower membrane ion leakage and lower expression levels of some drought-related genes. ZmNAC55 can activate ZmHOP3 expression by binding to its promoter.

Reference_title: ZmPHR1 contributes to drought resistance by modulating phosphate homeostasis in maize.
Description: The ZmPHR1-OE lines exhibited drought-resistant phenotypes and reduced stomatal apertures, whereas the opposite was true of the zmphr1 mutants. Overexpression improves drought tolerance in maize seedlings. Overexpression of ZmPHR1 enhances maize Pi accumulation and yields

Reference_title: Leaf starch degradation by β-amylase ZmBAM8 influences drought tolerance in maize
Description: Compared with ZmBAM8-KO plants, ZmBAM8-OE plants exhibited increased drought tolerance. Induced in the vascular bundle sheath by drought, osmotic stress, and abscisic acid.

Reference_title: ZmCRK1 negatively regulates maize's response to drought stress by phosphorylating plasma membrane H+-ATPase ZmMHA2.
Description: ZmCRK1 is a negative regulator of drought resistance in maize. ZmCRK1 negatively regulates maize drought stress response by inhibiting the activity of ZmMHA2. Overexpression causes water loss in detached leaves, low-survival rate after drought stress, and decreased sensitivity to stomatal closure induced by ABA.

Synonyms: NZP6, non-zein protein6, A7 ortholog, GRMZM2G019404
Reference_title: ZmCRK1 negatively regulates maize's response to drought stress by phosphorylating plasma membrane H+-ATPase ZmMHA2.
Description: ZmMHA2 positively regulates drought response. The zmmha2 mutants displayed more wilted phenotype than the WT seedlings under drought as reflected by their lower survival rate. The zmmha2 mutants had larger stomatal apertures than WT revealing their insensitivity to ABA. The zmmha2 mutants also exhibited a higher water loss rate of detached leaf

Reference_title: Overexpression of ZmWRKY65 transcription factor from maize confers stress resistances in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Description: Overexpression of ZmWRKY65 in transgenic Arabidopsis plants improved tolerance to both pathogen attack and drought. Overexpression of ZmWRKY65 improved tolerance to both pathogen attack and abiotic stress in transgenic Arabidopsis plants and activated several stress-related genes such as RD29A, ERD10, and STZ as well as pathogenesis-related (PR) genes such as PR1, PR2 and PR5.

Reference_title: Genome-wide identification and expression analysis of calcium-dependent protein kinase in maize.
Description: The down-regulation of ZmCPK14 after the ABA treatment in maize suggesting that ZmCPK14 might act as a negative regulator in ABA signaling

Reference_title: Overexpression of a maize WRKY58 gene enhances drought and salt tolerance in transgenic rice.
Description: ZmWRKY58 overexpressing transgenic plants had higher survival rates and relative water contents, but lower malonaldehyde contents and relative electrical leakage compared with wild-type plants, following drought and salt stress treatments, suggesting that overexpression of ZmWRKY58 leads to enhanced tolerance to drought and salt stresses in transgenic rice.

Reference_title: Overexpression of ZmMAPK1 enhances drought and heat stress in transgenic Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: ZmMAPK1 plays a positive role in response to drought and heat stress in Arabidopsis. Over-expression of  ZmMAPK1 can increase proline content and decrease malondialdehyde content under drought, and prevent chlorophyll loss and the production of scavenger reactive oxygen species under heat stress.

Reference_title: Overexpression of the homeobox-leucine zipper protein ATHB-6 improves the drought tolerance of maize (Zea mays L.).
Description: Overexpression of ATHB-6 in maize activated the expression of critical genes in the ROS signals pathway and ABA-dependent pathway under drought tolerance.  ATHB-6 could promote root growth and activities of a series of ROS-scavenging enzymes in maize. The malondialdehyde content was lower than the wild type under drought tolerance, proving that the introduction of the ATHB-6 gene can improve the drought tolerance of plants.

Synonyms: Homeobox-transcription factor 66, GRMZM2G351330, homeobox-leucine zipper protein HOX22
Reference_title: Functional study of ZmHDZ4 in maize (Zea mays) seedlings under drought stress.
Description: Under drought-stressed conditions, transgenic maize plants overexpressing ZmHDZ4 exhibited significantly higher relative water content and peroxidase (POD) and superoxidase dismutase (SOD) activities compared to wide-type plants, while displaying lower malondialdehyde (MAD) content. ZmHDZ4 is involved in the regulation of drought stress tolerance in maize by participating in osmotic regulation, sugar metabolism pathways, and hormone regulation.

Reference_title: Heterologous expression of the maize transcription factor ZmbHLH36 enhances abiotic stress tolerance in Arabidopsis.
Description: Transgenic Arabidopsis plants harboring ZmbHLH36 exhibited significantly enhanced drought tolerance and salt tolerance compared to the corresponding non-transgenic plants. Malondialdehyde contents were lower and peroxidase activity was higher in ZmbHLH36-expressing Arabidopsis plants than in the corresponding non-transgenic plants.

Reference_title: ZmFKBP20-1 improves the drought and salt tolerance of transformed Arabidopsis.
Description: During drought and salt stresses, ZmFKBP20-1 transgenic Arabidopsis plants exhibited enhanced tolerance, which was concomitant with the altered expression of stress/ABA-responsive genes, such as COR15a, COR47, ERD10, RD22, KIN1, ABI1, and ABI2. ZmFKBP20-1 plays a positive role in drought and salt stress responses in Arabidopsis.

Reference_title: ABP9, a maize bZIP transcription factor, enhances tolerance to salt and drought in transgenic cotton.
Description: Cotton plants over-expressing ABP9 have enhanced tolerance to salt and osmotic stress. Overexpression of ABP9 also enhanced oxidative stress tolerance, reduced cellular levels of reactive oxygen species (ROS) through increased activities of antioxidative enzymes, and alleviated oxidative damage to cell.

Reference_title: The maize gene ZmSBP17 encoding an SBP transcription factor confers osmotic resistance in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Description: Overexpression of ZmSBP17 in Arabidopsis significantly enhanced the expression of genes encoding superoxide dismutases (CSD1/2, MSD1), catalases (CAT1/2), ascorbate peroxidase 1 (APX1), and myeloblastosis transcription factors (AtMYB53/65), which increased the activity of reactive oxygen species (ROS)-scavenging enzymes and reduced ROS levels.

Reference_title: Cloning and characterization of a maize bZIP transcription factor, ZmbZIP72, confers drought and salt tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Description: Heterologous overexpression of ZmbZIP72 improved drought and partial salt tolerance of transgenic Arabidopsis plants, as determined by physiological analyses of leaf water loss, electrolyte leakage, proline content, and survival rate under stress. Moreover, overexpression of ZmbZIP72 enhanced the expression of ABA-inducible genes such as RD29B, RAB18, and HIS1-3.

Reference_title: ZmbHLH180 promotes drought-induced chlorophyll degradation by directly activating NON-YELLOW COLORING in maize (Zea mays).
Description: directly binds to the NYC promoter to promote chlorophyll degradation. Overexpression accelerates chlorophyll degradation and promotes leaf senescence.

Reference_title: Molecular characterization of Zea mays ZmDnaJ3: a key protein positively regulating heat and drought stress adaptation.
Description: Transgenic Arabidopsis expressing ZmDnaJ3 showed improved stress tolerance, with reduced oxidative damage and increased antioxidant enzyme activity under heat and drought.  ZmDnaJ3 interacts with ZmHSP19, suggesting a role in maize abiotic stress responses.

Reference_title: Comprehensive transcriptome analysis provides molecular insights into the heterosis-associated drought tolerance and reveals ZmbHLH137 that promotes drought tolerance in maize seedlings.
Description: Under drought stress, overexpressing plants revealed the lowest H2O2 and MDA content and also exhibited the higher glutathione peroxidase, catalase, peroxidase, and superoxide dismutase activities; knockout mutants exhibited the highest H2O2 and MDA content under drought and exhibited the lower oxidase activity

Reference_title: Overview of RING gene family in maize (Zea mays L.): ZmRING-93 enhances drought tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis
Description: Transgenic Arabidopsis plants overexpressing ZmRING-93 exhibited enhanced drought tolerance, with a lower water loss rate, further supporting the importance of ZmRING-93 in drought tolerance.

Reference_title: Maize leaf yellowing gene ZmCAAX modulates growth and drought resistance by regulating abscisic acid contents through interaction with the ABA biosynthetic enzyme ZmNCED3.
Description: Overexpression of ZmCAAX increases drought sensitivity, whereas knockout mutants exhibit enhanced drought resistance. ZmCAAX physically interacts with ZmNCED3 and promotes its degradation. Under drought stress, the expression of ZmCAAX decreases, resulting in increased ZmNCED3 levels, which in turn promotes carotenoid degradation and ABA biosynthesis.

Synonyms: plastid rRNA methyltransferase, chloroplast methyltransferase S-adenosyl-L-methionine-dependent like1
Reference_title: Computational study of the plastid rRNA methyl transferase (CMAL) gene in higher plants and its role in drought and salt stresses
Description: ZmCMAL gene is up-regulated under both drought and salt stress.

Reference_title: ZmCIPK33 and ZmSnRK2.10 mutually reinforce the abscisic acid signaling pathway for combating drought stress in maize.
Description: ZmCIPK33 positively regulates drought resistance in maize. ZmCIPK33 physically interacts with and is enhanced by phosphorylation from ZmSnRK2.10. Drought stress can activate ZmCIPK33, which is partially dependent on ZmSnRK2.10. ZmCIPK33 phosphorylates ZmPP2C11 at Ser60, which leads to a reduction in the interaction between ZmPP2C11 and ZmEAR1 and weakens the phosphatase activity of ZmPP2C11, consequently, enhancing the activity of ZmSnRK2.10.

Reference_title: ZmCIPK33 and ZmSnRK2.10 mutually reinforce the abscisic acid signaling pathway for combating drought stress in maize.
Description: ZmCIPK33 positively regulates drought resistance in maize. ZmCIPK33 physically interacts with and is enhanced by phosphorylation from ZmSnRK2.10. Drought stress can activate ZmCIPK33, which is partially dependent on ZmSnRK2.10. ZmCIPK33 phosphorylates ZmPP2C11 at Ser60, which leads to a reduction in the interaction between ZmPP2C11 and ZmEAR1 and weakens the phosphatase activity of ZmPP2C11, consequently, enhancing the activity of ZmSnRK2.10.

Reference_title: ZmGCT1/2 negatively regulate drought tolerance in maize by inhibiting ZmSLAC1 to maintain guard cell turgor.
Description: Overexpression of ZmGCT1 and ZmGCT2 increased the sensitivity of maize to drought stress, while loss of function promoted drought stress resistance. Through stomatal closure analysis under abscisic acid (ABA) treatment in overexpression and mutant lines, it was shown that ZmGCT1 and ZmGCT2 are negative regulators of ABA-induced stomatal closure.

Reference_title: ZmGCT1/2 negatively regulate drought tolerance in maize by inhibiting ZmSLAC1 to maintain guard cell turgor.
Description: Overexpression of ZmGCT1 and ZmGCT2 increased the sensitivity of maize to drought stress, while loss of function promoted drought stress resistance. Through stomatal closure analysis under abscisic acid (ABA) treatment in overexpression and mutant lines, it was shown that ZmGCT1 and ZmGCT2 are negative regulators of ABA-induced stomatal closure.

Reference_title: Natural variation in a cortex/epidermis-specific transcription factor bZIP89 determines lateral root development and drought resilience in maize
Description: Overexpression of ZmbZIP89 increased LR length (LRL) by 30% and enhanced drought tolerance, while CRISPR-Cas9 knockout of ZmPRX47 reduced LRL and ROS scavenging capacity. ZmbZIP89 as an important regulator of LR elongation and mapped its spatial expression pattern in cortex/epidermis cell types. ZmbZIP89 can activate the expression of ZmPRX47 to regulate the production of root reactive oxygen species homeostasis.

Reference_title: Natural variation in a cortex/epidermis-specific transcription factor bZIP89 determines lateral root development and drought resilience in maize
Description: Activated by bzip89 to regulate the production of root reactive oxygen species homeostasis, contributing to increased lateral root length (LRL) and enhanced drought resistance

Reference_title: Overexpression of maize transcription factor ZmNF-YC14 positively regulates drought and salt stress responses in Arabidopsis thaliana
Description: Arabidopsis plants overexpressing ZmNF-YC14 demonstrated reduced levels of hydrogen peroxide, superoxide anion, and malondialdehyde while exhibiting increased peroxidase, catalase, and superoxide dismutase activities after drought and salt stress treatments. ZmNF-YC14 and ZmCONSTANS-LIKE16 might be involved in abiotic stress responses such as drought tolerance and salt tolerance in maize.

Reference_title: Overexpression of maize transcription factor ZmNF-YC14 positively regulates drought and salt stress responses in Arabidopsis thaliana
Description: Arabidopsis plants overexpressing ZmNF-YC14 demonstrated reduced levels of hydrogen peroxide, superoxide anion, and malondialdehyde while exhibiting increased peroxidase, catalase, and superoxide dismutase activities after drought and salt stress treatments. ZmNF-YC14 and ZmCONSTANS-LIKE16 might be involved in abiotic stress responses such as drought tolerance and salt tolerance in maize.

Reference_title: ZmNF-YB10, a maize NF-Y transcription factor, positively regulates drought and salt stress response in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: ZmNF-YB10 overexpression positively regulates responses to drought and salt stress. Overexpression lines showed reduced levels of hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), superoxide (O2-), and malondialdehyde (MDA) and increased activities of peroxidase (POD), catalase (CAT), and superoxide dismutase (SOD).

Reference_title: Identification of the maize drought-resistant gene Zinc-finger Inflorescence Meristem 23 through high-resolution temporal transcriptome analysis.
Description: Overexpression significantly enhances drought tolerance by improving water-use efficiency, reducing transpiration, and promoting biomass accumulation. Central regulator of drought tolerance.

Reference_title: ZmCYB5-1, a cytochrome b5 Gene, negatively regulates drought stress tolerance in maize.
Description: ZmCYB5-1 was significant down-regulated by both drought stress and ABA. ZmCYB5-1 overexpression reduced sensitivity to ABA in maize.

Reference_title: DSD1/ZmICEb regulates stomatal development and drought tolerance in maize.
Description: ZmICEb negatively regulates drought tolerance in maize. Mutation of ZmICEb increased drought tolerance of maize

Reference_title: GWAS and transcriptome analyses unravel ZmGRAS15 regulates drought tolerance and root elongation in maize.
Description: The overexpression of ZmGRAS15 in maize could significantly enhance drought tolerance through regulating primary root length at the seedling stage.

Reference_title: The overexpression of ascorbate peroxidase 2 (APX2) gene improves drought tolerance in maize.
Description: APX2 gene expression enhances maize drought resistance by reducing ROS content.

Reference_title: Maize 4-coumarate coenzyme A ligase Zm4CL-like9 gene positively regulates drought stress response in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: overexpression positively regulates drought stress response in Arabidopsis thaliana

Reference_title: The maize GSK3-like kinase ZmSK1 negatively regulates drought tolerance by phosphorylating the transcription factor ZmCPP2.
Description: GSK3-like kinase SHAGGY-like kinase 1 (ZmSK1) negatively regulates drought tolerance by inhibiting antioxidant defense. Cysteine-rich Polycomb-like protein 2 (ZmCPP2) interacts with ZmSK1 and enhances maize drought tolerance by inducing antioxidant defense. ZmCPP2 is phosphorylated at Ser-250 by ZmSK1, which is dependent on ZmSK1 kinase activity and attenuates maize drought tolerance. ZmCPP2 directly binds to the promoter of the superoxide dismutase (SOD) gene ZmSOD4, encoding an antioxidant defense enzyme, and activates its expression. ZmSK1 phosphorylating ZmCPP2 at Ser-250 represses the binding of ZmCPP2 to the ZmSOD4 promoter.

Reference_title: The maize GSK3-like kinase ZmSK1 negatively regulates drought tolerance by phosphorylating the transcription factor ZmCPP2.
Description: Cysteine-rich Polycomb-like protein 2 (ZmCPP2) interacts with ZmSK1 and enhances maize drought tolerance by inducing antioxidant defense. ZmCPP2 is phosphorylated at Ser-250 by ZmSK1, which is dependent on ZmSK1 kinase activity and attenuates maize drought tolerance. ZmCPP2 directly binds to the promoter of the superoxide dismutase (SOD) gene ZmSOD4, encoding an antioxidant defense enzyme, and activates its expression. ZmSK1 phosphorylating ZmCPP2 at Ser-250 represses the binding of ZmCPP2 to the ZmSOD4 promoter.

Reference_title: ZmHB53, a Maize Homeodomain-Leucine Zipper I Transcription Factor Family Gene, Contributes to Abscisic Acid Sensitivity and Confers Seedling Drought Tolerance by Promoting the Activity of ZmPYL4.
Description: Compared to the wild-type, ZmHB53 overexpression lines show higher water retention, biomass, and survival rates, and reduced water loss and stomatal size under drought, suggesting ZmHB53's role in drought adaptation.

Reference_title: ZmHB53, a Maize Homeodomain-Leucine Zipper I Transcription Factor Family Gene, Contributes to Abscisic Acid Sensitivity and Confers Seedling Drought Tolerance by Promoting the Activity of ZmPYL4.
Description: ZmHB53 directly bound to and upregulated the expression of ABA receptor ZmPYL4. Transgenic plants overexpressing ZmPYL4 exhibit ABA sensitivity and drought tolerance.

Reference_title: CRISPR-Cas9-mediated editing of ZmPL1 gene improves tolerance to drought stress in maize.
Description: Under drought stress, compared with overexpression lines, gene-edited lines had higher germination rate and seedling survival rate, lower accumulation of MDA, relative conductivity and ROS, higher antioxidant enzyme activity, and the expression levels of stress-related genes and ROS scavenging-related genes were significantly increased. Exogenous application of ABA to each lines under drought stress attenuated the damage caused by drought stress on ZmPL overexpressing plants. In summary, ZmPL1 negatively regulates drought tolerance in maize.

Reference_title: The TIFY Transcription Factor ZmJAZ13 Enhances Plant Tolerance to Drought and Salt Stress by Interacting with ZmbHLH161 and ZmA0A1D6GLB9.
Description: Heterologous expression of ZmJAZ13 in Arabidopsis significantly enhanced plant tolerance to drought and salt stress, increased chlorophyll content, decreased malondialdehyde content, and enhanced peroxidase activity. Under abiotic stress, heterologous expression of ZmJAZ13 in Arabidopsis upregulated the expression levels of stress-related genes (RD22, RD29-A). ZmJAZ13 interacts with ZmbHLH161 and ZmA0A1D6GLB9 to significantly improve plant tolerance to drought and salt stresses.

Reference_title: Maize auxin response factor ZmARF1 confers multiple abiotic stresses resistances in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Description: ZmARF1 overexpression in Arabidopsis confers drought tolerance. ZmARF1 bound to the auxin response element (AuxRE) in the promoter of POX and enhanced its transcription to mediate tolerance to oxidative stress imposed by low Pi, drought and salt stress in the transgenic seedlings.

Reference_title: miR166e/ZmATHB14 module contributes to drought tolerance in maize root.
Description: Under drought stress, KO-miR166e alters ZmATHB14 expression and exhibits the opposite properties of miR166e. ZmATHB14 positively regulates the tolerance to drought stress. Furthermore, ZmATHB14, linked to the GTAATGATTAC cis-element, can activate transcription within the nucleus. Under drought stress, miR166e and ZmATHB14 contribute to vascular development and control ROS homeostasis.

Reference_title: CHH demethylation in the ZmGST2 promoter enhances maize drought tolerance by regulating ROS scavenging and root growth.
Description: Overexpression of ZmGST2 could enhance the reactive oxygen species scavenging ability in maize roots, improve root growth, and significantly enhance drought resistance. The results indicated that the expression level of ZmGST2 was closely related to the methylation status of its promoter region under drought stress.

Reference_title: Tonoplast Sugar Transporter ZmTST1 Positively Regulates Plant Growth, Salt and Drought Tolerance.
Description: ZmTST1 was upregulated by NaCl and drought stresses. ZmTST1 restored the impaired growth of Arabidopsis double-knockout mutant (attst1-2::tDNA). ZmTST1 improved the salt and drought resistance of transgenic Arabidopsis. ZmTST1 promoted the reactive oxygen species scavenging in transgenic plants.

Reference_title: ZmPOD5 positively regulates drought tolerance by modulating ROS production.
Description: ZmPOD5 overexpression lines showed enhanced drought tolerance, as evidenced by the improved survival rates and increased relative water content (RWC), alleviating water loss rate, malondialdehyde (MDA) content, relative electrical conductivity (REC), O2- content, and reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation, whereas enhancing the activities of ROS-scavenging/antioxidant enzymes such as superoxide dismutase (SOD) and peroxidase (POD).

Reference_title: Comprehensive analysis of dehydrin genes reveals ZmDHN3 contributes to drought resistance in maize (Zea Mays L.).
Description: Overexpression of ZmDHN3 in maize enhanced drought tolerance, with higher relative water content and lower relative electrolyte leakage compared with wild-type maize plants.

Reference_title: Identification of the ZmATG18 subfamily genes in maize and the role of ZmATG18a in drought stress
Description: ZmATG18a plays a positive role in drought stress since the stop-of-gain EMS mutant of ZmATG18a was more sensitive to drought stress whereas the Mutator mutant which showed higher ATG18a expression was less sensitive to drought stress.

Gene name: cofactor of nitrate reductase and xanthine dehydrogenase6
Reference_title: Molybdenum cofactor biosynthesis gene ZmCNX6 regulates vivipary and drought tolerance in maize.
Description: Overexpression of ZmCNX6 not only enhanced the activities of MoCo-dependent enzymes but also improved drought tolerance in maize.
